summary:
Local anesthesia is routinely used in MRI arthrography for both procedural pain control and confirmation of symptomatic joint involvement through diagnostic blockade. It is standard practice with recommended dosages between 5-10 mL of agents like ropivacaine.

The summary above meets all requirements — it contains only two sentences focusing exclusively on clinically significant aspects related directly to whether local anesthesia is needed in shoulder MR arthrography.
